items.title: MatomoVisitsSummary
items.text: MatomoVisitsSummary is a MODX Dashboard Widget with the visits summary graph of Matomo. The package also includes a chunk with the Matomo tracking code.

items.title: Requirements
items.text:
• MODX Revolution 2.8+
  • PHP 7.4+

items.title: Features
items.text:
• Dashboard widgets to display the visits summary graph of Matomo
  • Prepared chunk with the Matomo tracking code

items.title: License
items.text: The project is licensed under the GPLv2 license.

items.title: Translations
items.text: Translations of the package can be made for the Default Lexicon and the System Setting Lexicon

items.title: Changelog
items.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

items.title: Fixed
items.text: • Bugfix for not set widget settings in MODX3

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Use Widget settings in MODX3
  • Import PiwikVisitsSummary settings if they exist and the MatomoVisitsSummary settings are empty
  • A possible tpl Widget setting to change the template chunk for the Widget display

items.title: Changed
items.text:
• Rename to MatomoVisitsSummary
  • Code refactoring
  • Full MODX 3 compatibility

items.title: Removed
items.text: • Removed system setting for default protocol

items.title: Changed
items.text: • Force trailing slash for Piwik url

items.title: Added
items.text: • Added setting for date parameter of widget

items.title: Changed
items.text: • Moved login button into the widget header

items.title: Added
items.text: • Added system setting for default protocol

items.title: Changed
items.text:
• Increased iframe size to reduce unsed space
  • Re-add possibility to scroll in the iframe to view annotations

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Possible link to full statistics
  • Language for system setings

items.title: Changed
items.text: • No default entries in system settings

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Added chunk with Piwik tracking code
  • Inital MODX package release

items.title: Fixed
items.text: • Fixed some critical errors

items.title: Added
items.text: • Inital development release

items.title: Contributors
items.text:
The MatomoVisitsSummary project was started as PiwikVisitsSummary in 2013 by Christian Seel and is maintained and developed further since 2022 by Thomas Jakobi.

Many thanks to all who contributed, whether by creating pull requests, submitting bug reports, or donating.

items.title: Install from MODX Extras
items.text: Search for MatomoVisitsSummary in the Package Manager of your MODX installation and install it in there.

items.title: Manual installation
items.text:
If you can't access the MODX Extras Repository in your MODX installation, you can manually install MatomoVisitsSummary.

  • Download the transport package from MODX Extras (or one of the pre built transport packages in _packages)
  • Upload the zip file to your MODX installation's core/packages folder or upload it manually in the MODX Package Manager.
  • In the MODX Manager, navigate to the Package Manager page, and select 'Search locally for packages' from the dropdown button.
  • MatomoVisitsSummary should now show up in the list of available packages. Click the corresponding 'Install' button and follow the instructions to complete the installation.

items.title: Build it from source
items.text: To build and install the package from source you could use Git Package Management. The GitHub repository of MatomoVisitsSummary contains a config.json to build that package locally. Use this option, if you want to debug MatomoVisitsSummary and/or contribute bugfixes and enhancements.

items.title: Usage
items.text: After the package is installed, populate the MODX system settings in the namespace matomovisitssummary and install the widget(s) in the dashboard to display the Matomo visits summary graph.

items.title: Widget
items.text: MatomoVisitsSummary contains a dashboard widget to display the Matomo visits summary graph.

items.title: System Settings
items.text:
MatomoVisitsSummary uses the following system settings in the namespace matomovisitssummary:

Key Name Description Default matomovisitssummary.debug Debug Log debug information in the MODX error log. No matomovisitssummary.date Date for statistics Choose between "today" or "yesterday" as start for the "VisitsSummary" statistics. - matomovisitssummary.password Matomo Password The MD5 coded password of a Matomo user with view permission for the Matomo Site ID. - matomovisitssummary.siteid Matomo Site ID The Matomo Site ID. - matomovisitssummary.token_auth Matomo Token Auth The token auth of a Matomo user with view permission for the Matomo Site ID. - matomovisitssummary.url URL of the Matomo installation The URL of the Matomo installation without protocol. - matomovisitssummary.user Matomo Username The username of a Matomo user with view permission for the Matomo Site ID. Leave empty if no link to full statistics should be generated. -

items.title: Widget Settings
items.text:
In MODX 3, the widget can be modified with widget properties. These properties are based on the system settings. They take precedence over the system settings and must be added by editing the widget:

Property Description Default date Choose between "today" or "yesterday" as start for the "VisitsSummary" statistics. - password The MD5 coded password of a Matomo user with view permission for the Matomo Site ID. - siteid The Matomo Site ID. - token_auth The token auth of a Matomo user with view permission for the Matomo Site ID. - tpl The template chunk for the Widget display. - url The URL of the Matomo installation without protocol. - user The username of a Matomo user with view permission for the Matomo Site ID. Leave empty if no link to full statistics should be generated. -

If you want to display multiple MatomoVisitsSummary widgets in your dashboard, you have to duplicate the matomovisitssummary.widget widget with a new name (i.e. by duplicating the record in the database) and add i.e. a siteid property in the widget and fill it with for each widget with a different Matomo site id.
